requirements.title: CLI entry point with configuration
requirements.description: The tool runs as a CLI command: `hall-monitor --url <discourse-url>`. It accepts a Discourse root URL as a required argument and optional flags for API key, output format, severity threshold, and category/tag filters. Configuration can also be read from a `.hall-monitor.json` file in the current directory.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI parses --url, --api-key, --api-username, --config, --json, --severity flags
Falls back to .hall-monitor.json if present and no flags given
Prints usage help with --help
Exits with code 1 and a clear message if no URL is provided

requirements.title: SQLite state database
requirements.description: A local SQLite database tracks which topics and posts have been seen, when the last check ran, and the results of prior analyses. This enables idempotent runs — each invocation processes only new or updated content since the last run.
acceptanceCriteria: Database is created automatically at ~/.hall-monitor/state.db on first run
Schema includes tables: seen_topics, seen_posts, analysis_results, run_log
seen_topics tracks topic_id, last_post_number, last_checked_at
Migrations run automatically on startup if schema is outdated
Database path is overridable via --db flag

requirements.title: Fetch latest topics from Discourse
requirements.description: Connect to a Discourse instance via its public JSON API and retrieve the latest topics. Support pagination to collect a configurable number of recent topics (default: 100). Handle HTTP errors, rate limiting (429 + Retry-After), and network failures gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /latest.json with pagination support
Respects rate limits: backs off on 429 using Retry-After header
Returns typed Topic[] array with id, title, slug, posts_count, views, like_count, created_at, last_posted_at, category_id, tags, excerpt
Supports optional API key auth via Api-Key and Api-Username headers
Logs HTTP errors to stderr without crashing

requirements.title: Fetch topic details and posts
requirements.description: For topics identified as potentially relevant, fetch the full topic with its posts using /t/{id}.json. Retrieve enough context for the LLM to classify — at minimum the original post and latest replies.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /t/{topic_id}.json to get full topic data
Retrieves the first post (OP) and up to 5 most recent replies
Strips HTML from post bodies to produce clean text for LLM input
Handles deleted/private topics gracefully (skip with warning)

requirements.title: Fetch topics by category and tag
requirements.description: Allow monitoring specific Discourse categories and/or tags instead of (or in addition to) the global latest feed. Users configure this via category slugs/IDs and tag names.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /c/{slug}/{id}.json for specified categories
Fetches /tag/{tag_name}.json for specified tags
Deduplicates topics that appear in multiple categories/tags
Falls back to /latest.json if no categories or tags are configured

requirements.title: Incremental fetch using stored state
requirements.description: On subsequent runs, only fetch topics that are new or have new posts since the last check. Use the SQLite state database to track the last seen state per topic.
acceptanceCriteria: Compares fetched topic list against seen_topics table
Identifies new topics (not in seen_topics) and updated topics (last_post_number increased)
Only fetches full details for new/updated topics
Updates seen_topics after successful processing
First run processes all fetched topics (no prior state)

requirements.title: Pre-filter topics before LLM analysis
requirements.description: Apply fast, cheap heuristics to filter out obvious noise before sending content to the LLM. This reduces API costs and latency. Heuristics include: skip topics with no replies and low views (likely spam/noise), skip topics older than a configurable window, skip topics in excluded categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Filters out topics with 0 replies and < 5 views (configurable thresholds)
Filters out topics older than 30 days by default (configurable)
Allows category exclusion list in config
Pre-filter is applied before any LLM API calls
Filtered topics are logged at debug level for transparency

requirements.title: LLM-powered topic classification
requirements.description: Send topic content (title, OP body, recent replies) to Claude for classification. The LLM assigns one of the defined alert categories (bug-report, regression, security, feature-request, pain-point, praise, trend, noise) and a severity level (critical, high, medium, low). Responses are structured/parsed into typed results.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls Claude API with a well-crafted system prompt defining the OSS maintainer context
Sends topic title + OP text + up to 5 reply excerpts as user message
Parses structured response into { category, severity, summary, reasoning }
Handles LLM API errors gracefully (retry once on 5xx, skip on persistent failure)
Uses Haiku model by default for cost efficiency, configurable to Sonnet for higher accuracy

requirements.title: Batch analysis to reduce API calls
requirements.description: Group multiple short topics into a single LLM request where possible. This reduces round-trips and cost. Each batch should stay within token limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Groups topics into batches of up to 5 (configurable)
Estimates token count to stay under model context window
Falls back to single-topic analysis for long topics
Maintains correct mapping from batch response to individual topics

requirements.title: Trend detection across topics
requirements.description: After individual classification, perform a second pass to detect trends — recurring themes, growing frustrations, or emerging feature requests that appear across multiple topics. This is a cross-topic analysis done at the end of a run.
acceptanceCriteria: Groups classified topics by category and extracts common themes
Sends grouped summaries to LLM for trend analysis
Outputs detected trends as separate items in the report
Only runs when >= 10 classified topics are available in a single run

requirements.title: Terminal report output
requirements.description: After analysis, print a human-readable report to stdout. Group findings by severity, show category labels, one-line summaries, and links back to the original forum topics. Use color and formatting for readability.
acceptanceCriteria: Groups findings by severity (critical first, then high, medium, low)
Each finding shows: [CATEGORY] Title — one-line summary (link)
Critical and high severity items are highlighted
Shows run summary: N topics checked, M findings, K new since last run
Respects NO_COLOR env var for CI environments

requirements.title: JSON output mode
requirements.description: Support --json flag to output the full analysis results as structured JSON to stdout. This enables integration with CI pipelines, dashboards, and other tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: Outputs valid JSON array of findings to stdout
Each finding includes: topicId, topicUrl, title, category, severity, summary, reasoning, detectedAt
JSON output is machine-parseable (no ANSI codes, no mixed stderr/stdout)
Non-finding output (progress, warnings) goes to stderr

requirements.title: Exit code reflects findings
requirements.description: Exit with different codes depending on what was found, enabling CI/automation to take action based on severity.
acceptanceCriteria: Exit 0: no findings above threshold
Exit 1: findings at or above configured severity threshold
Exit 2: runtime error (network, API, config)
Severity threshold is configurable via --severity flag

requirements.title: Init command to generate config file
requirements.description: Provide a `hall-monitor init` subcommand that interactively generates a .hall-monitor.json config file. Prompts for the Discourse URL, optional API key, categories to watch, and severity threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs as `hall-monitor init`
Prompts for Discourse URL and validates it returns a valid /site.json
Prompts for optional API key
Fetches and displays available categories for selection
Writes .hall-monitor.json to current directory
Warns if file already exists and asks to overwrite

requirements.title: Anthropic API key configuration
requirements.description: The Anthropic API key for LLM analysis must be configurable via multiple sources with clear precedence: CLI flag > environment variable (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) > config file.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads from --anthropic-api-key flag first
Falls back to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
Falls back to anthropicApiKey in .hall-monitor.json
Exits with clear error message if no key is found
Never logs or displays the API key
